TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Elven Conclaves Faction.

Translations.Comment: Соборы (Sobory - Cathedrals/Councils) conveys the organized, council-like nature of Elven society. Maintains dignity and fantasy tone.
Копьеносцы (Spear-bearers) is more specific than just 'копья' (spears) and fits military unit naming conventions.
Городская Стража (City Guard) is a compound construction that maintains the defensive role and military formality.
Direct translation conveying elite skill and mastery, fitting for Elven warriors in a wargame setting.
Лучники (Archers) is the standard term. Elves are famous for their archery; this captures that heritage perfectly.
Дозорные (Sentries/Watchers) implies vigilance and protective duty, fitting for their role.
Баллист (Ballista operator) is consistent with siege weapon terminology in fantasy wargames.
Копьеносцы на Конях (Lance riders/cavalry) elegantly conveys mounted cavalry troops with martial elegance.
Рейдеры (Raiders) captures the swift cavalry that strikes and withdraws. Conveys aggressive mobility.
Боевая Колесница (War Chariot) is classic military vehicle terminology appropriate for fantasy wargames.
Огромный Орёл (Giant Eagle) is direct translation. Majestic creature fitting for Elven air forces.
Драконьи Рыцари (Dragon Knights) conveys elite dragon-riding warriors; the compound form captures both mount and rider nobility.
Древний (Ancient) emphasizes age and power. Conveys the wisdom and majesty befitting elder dragons.
Грифон is the standard Russian term for gryphon/griffin. Mythological creature appropriate for Elven forces.
Дворянин (Nobleman) is a traditional term for aristocratic leaders fitting for Elven nobility.
Маг is the standard Russian term for mage/wizard in fantasy. Conveys magical expertise and mystical power.
Боевой Маг (Battle Mage) conveys the martial-magical hybrid warrior. Alternative: Чародей-Клинок (Sorcerer-Blade). Chosen form emphasizes both combat and magic equally.
